selected_title: POLAND – EUROPEAN MODEL OF DECENTRALIZATION REFORM
question: Where does “POLAND – EUROPEAN MODEL OF DECENTRALIZATION REFORM” support or contradict the causal account advanced by “Analysis on Two Thoughts on the Central and Local Decentralization Before and After the 8th CPC National Congress”?

evidence_spans.text: Poland, a country become free, like Romania, has joined the process of democratic transition, the first reform via decentralization has been developed since 1990 and could serve as a model in the implementation of the decentralization reform, establishing a true institutional and financial decentralization. What we propose in this article is to analyze the way administrative-territorial decentralization of Poland, a country that was governed through centralized communist systems similar to Romania, has performed in implementing decentralization and local autonomy in the governance process of the local public administration.
